speaker: interviewer
text: 1994. Interview with--

speaker: interviewer
text: Victor Lewis.

speaker: interviewer
text: Victor Lewis. OK, Victor, go ahead. Tell me what this is.

speaker: interviewee
text: This, this is menorah for departed Jews, six million Jews. This I had idea to make it when I was in shop. I had the shop 20 years ago. And you see here, there are all kind of concentration camp. Flossenburg, Bergen-Belsen, Theresienstadt, Gross-Rosen, Mauthausen, Buchenwald, Majdanek, Treblinka, Belzec, Plaszow, Cracow Ghetto, Warsaw Ghetto, Auschwitz, Birkenau--

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you--

speaker: interviewee
text: Sobibor--

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you turn it slowly for me?

speaker: interviewee
text: This--

speaker: interviewer
text: The other way please.

speaker: interviewee
text: The other way.

speaker: interviewer
text: Slowly. Go on.

speaker: interviewee
text: Budzyn, Stutthof, Sachsenhausen, Ravensbruck, Riga, Chelmno, Nordhausen. And it's written never to forget in English. And [INAUDIBLE]. This means never to forget. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Now, when did you make this?

speaker: interviewee
text: Around 20 years ago.

speaker: interviewer
text: You made this in your shop?

speaker: interviewee
text: In my shop, right.

speaker: interviewer
text: You forgot one thing. The-- to signify the barbed wire.

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes, tell me what that is.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is barbed wires from concentration camp.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is it actually--

speaker: interviewee
text: Is it actually barbed wire from a concentration camp? No, I made the barbed wire.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: And this I gonna probably give to some museum. I don't know which one, here in New York or in Washington. Probably in New York. [PAUSES FOR 12 SECONDS] [INAUDIBLE]. [PAUSES FOR 22 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, Start again. Tell me where was this picture? What country is Jordanow? Is that Poland?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Poland. This was on vacation on July 11, 1937. My brother Leo, my mother Bertha, me, and my youngest brother Jacob. [PAUSES FOR 9 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, just one second. Tell me now-- tell me-- this is-- this is Cracow, Poland, 1938.

speaker: interviewee
text: Oh, you see it?

speaker: interviewer
text: Yes, I can read it. Now what is your last name? This is your last name. Leserkiewicz.

speaker: interviewee
text: Leserkiewicz.

speaker: interviewer
text: Left to right. Bertha--

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] Abraham, and Lola Leserkiewicz.

speaker: interviewer
text: These are Mr. Lewis's parents and his sister.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, and here's the whole family.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, please tell me about this.

speaker: interviewee
text: From left to right, my brother Leo; my mother, Bertha; my father, Abraham; my sister, Greta; myself, Victor, and the youngest brother Jacob Leserkiewicz.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where?

speaker: interviewee
text: In some kind of a--

speaker: interviewer
text: Country home?

speaker: interviewee
text: Country home, yeah. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Before the war?

speaker: interviewee
text: Before the war, '36.

speaker: interviewer
text: '36. OK, tell me who this is, please.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my father, Abraham Leserkiewicz, made way before the war. I don't remember when.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my mother Bertha Leserkiewicz, also before the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: In Cracow.

speaker: interviewer
text: Cracow, OK. Please tell me about this.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my sister, Greta Leserkiewicz, made before the war, two or three years before the war. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] OK, this is my youngest brother, Jacob Leserkiewicz. This is made before the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: Just one moment.

speaker: interviewer
text: How old is he?

speaker: interviewer
text: How old is he?

speaker: interviewer
text: In that photo.

speaker: interviewee
text: On this here? Ten, eleven.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is me, Victor Leserkiewicz, 1936 in Cracow. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: One moment please. [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: OK, this my wife and me, Victor Leserkiewicz, Regina Steiner, in 1938 in Cracow on Vistula River.

speaker: interviewer
text: Just one moment. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] I want to do this in a close up, too. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] Isn't that sweet? [PAUSES FOR 14 SECONDS] [INAUDIBLE]

speaker: interviewee
text: Do I leave it?

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, completely.

speaker: interviewer
text: Oh, yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is-- this is a one Reichsmark for good working, of hard working. I don't for how long, a month or life.

speaker: interviewer
text: Now tell me what could you buy with something like this?

speaker: interviewee
text: A cigarette.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's all?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's all.

speaker: interviewer
text: That's all that it was worth or that's all that was available?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's all it was worth.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mr. Lewis, what-- what concentration camp was that?

speaker: interviewee
text: Gross-Rosen.

speaker: interviewer
text: Can you read the whole top line for us, please?

speaker: interviewee
text: Sure. Concentration lager Gross-Rosen, Pramienschein, number 63474, worth Reichsmark, one Reichsmark.

speaker: interviewer
text: Go ahead and-- go ahead.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is the other side and my name on it. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Now why did you not use it? Why did you-- how did you keep this through the whole war? How did you still have this today?

speaker: interviewee
text: Because I saved this.

speaker: interviewer
text: You didn't want-- you wanted to save it.

speaker: interviewee
text: That's right.

speaker: interviewer
text: Very interesting. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] OK, Victor, please tell me what this book is.

speaker: interviewee
text: This book is My story is in the book. I don't remember the page number.

speaker: interviewer
text: Page 117.

speaker: interviewee
text: Page 117. [PAUSES FOR 9 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Make the correction and tell me what page is this on?

speaker: interviewee
text: Page is 217. [PAUSES FOR 14 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Go ahead and tell me who this is and point to yourself.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a team in [INAUDIBLE], Austria. And I am fifth from the left.

speaker: interviewer
text: Just, you can actually go ahead and point to it. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] Right there.

speaker: interviewee
text: That's me.

speaker: interviewer
text: Fifth from left. OK. I'll see. Hold on. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] We've got some glare there. Go ahead. Please point  to yourself.

speaker: interviewee
text: That's me, Victor Lewis Leserkiewicz.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is your finger on it or the one next to your finger?

speaker: interviewee
text: In front of the finger.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, OK, good. That's good. That's fine. Thank you. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] OK. Don't touch it, but just tell me about what this is.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] what is it? A pass.

speaker: interviewer
text: Identification.

speaker: interviewee
text: Identification card as a police--

speaker: interviewer
text: Police chief.

speaker: interviewee
text: Chief police of the camp--

speaker: interviewer
text: Bad Ischl.

speaker: interviewee
text: No, camp-- DP camp, in Bad Ischl.

speaker: interviewer
text: A moment.

speaker: interviewer
text: And this should have gone with the [INAUDIBLE]. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: That's not important. That's not important. I already showed them the--

speaker: interviewer
text: Just one moment.

speaker: interviewer
text: You lost the picture here, too.

speaker: interviewee
text: No, I took the picture out.

speaker: interviewer
text: This is-- this is the other side of the police pass, where he was the chief of police in the DP camp. All right, I'm sorry. Start that again.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is in 1946 in Vienna in the sport club, Jewish Hakoah Club.

speaker: interviewer
text: You're in your cleats. OK. OK, now, tell me. This is 1946 in--

speaker: interviewee
text: In Vienna. This is the-- [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Membership card.

speaker: interviewee
text: Membership card, right. You're right. This is a membership card of Hakoah, Vienna in 1946. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: One moment. [PAUSES FOR 11 SECONDS] Please tell me what this is.

speaker: interviewee
text: This is a membership card of Selfaid of Jewish Former Concentration Camp Inmates in Austria. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Just one moment. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: From Vienna. From the international refuge. International refuge.

speaker: interviewer
text: Just one second so we don't confuse it with this one. This one is the inside to the last image. This is the refugee of-- of a-- of a concentration camp identity-- identity card.

speaker: interviewee
text: Right. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And go right ahead.

speaker: interviewee
text: 1965, In September, I was in Kiel, Germany to a-- a-- to a-- how you say--

speaker: interviewer
text: Conference?

speaker: interviewee
text: No. In-- in court. To a court hearing about SS-- [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] SS name Martin Fellenz who sent all Jews from Cracow Ghetto to [INAUDIBLE]. He was the chief in the exhibition. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, I have to get some images of each of these pieces of text.

speaker: interviewee
text: What do you mean?

speaker: interviewer
text: Don't worry about it. I'm just getting all the material on tape. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] I'm going to digitalize it so I don't have to do it for-- just doing sections. [PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Are you getting it all in? I'm getting it in sections. Yeah, I made sure I double lined. I doubled. [PAUSES FOR 14 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. Now, maybe they can digitalize that. But-- but if they can't, I've got-- I've got each section--

speaker: interviewer
text: Right. But I'm sure they can. I mean even now just on regular computer, you can make each part--

speaker: interviewee
text: But it's never going to be as clear as if they were in close ups. [SIDE CONVERSATION]
